week_01: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | 9-1 demolition in Week 1, balanced offense with four HR hitters, three solid SP performances — the most complete team in the league right now | up
2. BaseballPro77 | Trout (6 R, 3 HR, 5 RBI, .625 OBP) and Murakami both went deep three times in a week; offense is historically dangerous and 8-2 reflects it | same

3. General Hospital | Tied despite 23 RBI, .414 OBP, and 6 HR — this roster is elite and the tie feels more like bad luck than a weakness; adding Kumar Rocker deepens an already strong rotation | same
4. North Correa | Chris Sale was untouchable (15 K, 0.75 WHIP, 2 QS), Ohtani contributed on both sides, and a 7-3 win over the Reapers shows legitimate depth | up
5. Athletic Park Dragons | Judge and Will Smith went deep, Sanchez was filthy (0.50 WHIP), and a 5-4 gritty win over Hiroshima shows this team can grind out victories | same
6. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Seven HR, three QS, and a 2.98 ERA in a tie — the pitching is real, the offense is emerging, and Brendan Donovan's multi-position versatility is a huge roster asset | up
7. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Alvarez and Aranda mashed, 43 K from the staff, but a 6.00 ERA and zero QS in 39 IP cost them dearly; adding Merrill Kelly is the right move | down
8. Hiroshima Radiation | Lost 4-5 despite 44 K and a 2.35 ERA — Skubal and Bradley were sharp, but Eovaldi's 9.64 ERA and a completely frozen stolen base column killed them | down
9. Albuquerque Bombers | Kyle Harrison's 0.33 WHIP and Yelich's .600 OBP were electric; four aggressive waiver adds signal a manager who knows this roster needs reinforcing | same
10. Benchwarmers | Lucky 7-3 win masks a thin roster, but Michael King (14 K, 1.64 ERA) and Spencer Arrighetti (8 K, 1.29 ERA) give them a real two-ace foundation | up
11. Canberra Wombats | Dylan Cease was sharp (1.69 ERA) but Aaron Nola got rocked (8.38 ERA); an 0-HR week in a power-scoring league is a brutal structural flaw | down
12. Riverside Roughnecks | Lost 1-9 in the most lopsided result of the week; Langeliers hit 3 HR but the pitching was historically bad (7.91 ERA, 0 QS, 19.1 IP) | down
13. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | 39 K is impressive but Matthew Boyd's 14.73 ERA and Tyler Wells' 10.80 ERA are disqualifying; only 1 HR all week in a power league is alarming | same
14. Buttville Skidmarks | DeLauter slugging 4 HR can't paper over a .271 OBP and 6.02 ERA — until the pitching stabilizes, this team will keep losing winnable matchups | same
week_02: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | League-best 40 R, 8-1 blowout, Drake Baldwin and Matt Olson both erupted — three straight top-tier weeks cements top spot | same
2. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | 9-0 sweep is the statement of the season; Ben Rice, Mason Miller, and a 0.84 WHIP rotation demand respect | up
3. General Hospital | 2.10 ERA, .363 OBP, and a 6-4 win despite -0.52 luck delta — this team is quietly undervalued and primed to surge | same
4. BaseballPro77 | Ugly offensive week (2 HR, .277 OBP) but pitching bailed them out; a 5-4 win is a 5-4 win and they remain elite on the mound | down
5. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Yordan Alvarez went nuclear (.600 OBP, 3 HR, 8 RBI), league-record-tying 77 K, and a 2.25 ERA — Merrill Kelly add could be pivotal | up
6. North Correa | Oneil Cruz (4 HR, 10 RBI) and Ohtani (.441 OBP) powered a 7-3 win; pitching ERA of 2.85 rounds out a balanced roster | same
7. Riverside Roughnecks | 38 runs, 10 HR, and a 7-3 victory — this team has quietly become one of the most explosive offenses in the league | up
8. Athletic Park Dragons | Lost 6-4 despite Judge, Wood, and Torres all contributing — thin pitching (46 IP) remains the Achilles heel | down
9. Hiroshima Radiation | 8-1 dismantling is a brutal wake-up call; Carlos Correa and Schwarber are fine but the team gave up 40 runs and only 1 category | down
10. Albuquerque Bombers | Lost 7-3 but Kyle Harrison (26 K, 1.65 ERA) was a revelation; aggressive waiver activity shows a manager fighting back | same
11. Canberra Wombats | Altuve (.577 OBP), Henderson (3 HR), and Walker (3 HR) all hit — but pitching (Seth Lugo 7.15 ERA) remains a disaster | up
12. North Correa already ranked — Buttville Skidmarks | Lost 7-3 despite solid pitching (3.19 ERA, 4 QS); offense is too passive and Reynolds' production is being wasted | down
13. Benchwarmers | Carroll was a one-man army but the roster around him is still thin; barely escaped with a 5-4 win against a struggling opponent | same
14. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | A 9-0 shutout loss with zero category wins is as bad as it gets; the offense (3 HR, 13 RBI) is an emergency | down
week_03: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Three straight top-tier category weeks, 2.11 ERA this week, Matt Olson (3 HR, 8 RBI) and Drake Baldwin anchoring a balanced roster that just won't quit | same
2. Hiroshima Radiation | 8-2 blowout win with 75 K, 6 QS, league-best pitching depth — Taj Bradley and McLean are an elite duo, bats quietly contributing | up
3. BaseballPro77 | Jordan Walker exploded for 5 HR and 6 RBI, Logan Gilbert was surgical, and this roster has multiple weeks of elite production — still a top-3 threat | same
4. Athletic Park Dragons | James Wood (3 HR, 8 RBI, .636 OBP) and Willson Contreras (3 HR, 11 RBI) both went nuclear; 6-3 win is comfortable; real contender | same
5. General Hospital | Manny Machado (3 HR, 11 RBI) and Cam Schlittler (15 K) are difference-makers but a 4-4 tie against Buttville when expected to win 1.54 is a frustrating step back | down
6. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Lost 8-2 despite Gunnar Henderson, Yordan Alvarez, and deGrom all having strong weeks — still a deep roster but badly outgunned by Hiroshima | down
7. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Ildemaro Vargas (.600 OBP) was a waiver gem, Masyn Winn adds real upside, and their pitching metrics remain solid despite the 5-4 loss | up
8. North Correa | Shohei Ohtani (4 HR, .529 OBP) and Chris Sale (22 K) are a star-studded core but the rest of the roster couldn't back them up enough in a 5-3 loss | same
9. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Kris Bubic's 0.43 WHIP and Brandon Woodruff returning are encouraging signs, but the offense still can't generate enough counting stats to compete consistently | same
10. Albuquerque Bombers | Four waiver additions including Josh Jung and Braxton Garrett show aggressive management; Kyle Harrison and Landen Roupp both pitched well in the 5-4 win | up
11. Riverside Roughnecks | A 6-3 win over Benchwarmers is a win, but zero quality starts and a 5.04 ERA from the rotation is a ceiling-limiting problem that won't go away on its own | same
12. Buttville Skidmarks | A 4-4 tie feels like a win for this roster — MacKenzie Gore's 0.60 WHIP and Elly De La Cruz's 4 SB are real weapons, but the rotation depth is dangerously thin | up

13. Canberra Wombats | Cal Raleigh, Henderson, and deGrom gave them one of the more impressive individual-player weeks in the league and they still got blown out — roster balance is the fatal flaw | down
14. Benchwarmers | Michael King (12 K, 3.00 ERA) continues to be wasted — no QS, 34 team strikeouts, and a 5.53 ERA mean even a 6-3 win can't hide how fragile this roster really is | same
week_04: 1. BaseballPro77 | Set league records in HR and RBI, Trout is in full MVP mode, and the pitching held a 2.33 ERA — no one else is playing at this level right now | same
2. General Hospital | Led the league with 8 category wins, 77.1 IP, 6 QS, and a 2.68 ERA; added Kumar Rocker to an already deep rotation that is becoming truly frightening | same
3. Sugar Land Skeeters | Dropped a 5-4 decision but remain the most consistent team in the league across all four weeks; Tatis, Happ, and Weathers give them multi-category upside nightly | same
4. Athletic Park Dragons | Judge's 6-HR week was jaw-dropping and Sánchez/Ober both delivered; this team is rounding into a genuine contender with pitching depth to match the offense | same
5. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | A league-leading 8 QS and 2.49 ERA remain the identity; Skenes' 0.90 ERA and smart waiver additions like Masyn Winn show an active, forward-thinking manager | same

6. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Ramirez and Alvarez each went off for 4 HR and the team won 6 categories — the talent is unquestionable, but a -0.61 luck delta is a brutal ongoing tax on a top-five roster | up

7. North Correa | Chris Sale's 0.69 ERA anchored the week and the Ohtani-Hoerner combo gives them a high floor; Wells and Rocchio upgrades at catcher and middle infield show smart roster management | same
8. Hiroshima Radiation | McLean and Eovaldi combined for 5 QS and sub-2.00 ERAs again, but a -0.39 luck delta and an offense that managed just 6 HR and 1 SB are real ceiling concerns | down
9. Albuquerque Bombers | Buxton's 3-HR week sparked a 5-4 win and the roster is actively being patched with four waiver additions; the activity is encouraging but the holes are real | same
10. Riverside Roughnecks | Tied when a win looked probable; Langeliers, Kurtz, and Misiorowski are legitimate assets but the team still can't string enough categories together consistently | same
11. Canberra Wombats | A tie beats a loss but Henderson's .189 OBP is alarming for a cornerstone player; the pitching inventory is thin and the Week 5 draw against Wonder Wharf is a tough ask | same

12. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Got steamrolled 7-2 by the league's hottest team; Ben Rice is a real piece but 33 hits and a .257 OBP league-wide signal a roster that needs significant offensive upgrades | down

13. Buttville Skidmarks | Reynolds posted a .500 OBP again and it meant nothing; the pitching posted a 4.87 ERA and the team managed just 3 category wins — the same structural problems, the same result | same

14. Benchwarmers | Michael King's 19 K effort is elite and real, but a +1.21 luck delta masking 4 HR, 45 K, and bottom-five offense means regression is coming fast unless major additions are made | same
